description: Initialize a variable on all zones in the range.

keywords.name: extra
keywords.description: Extra variable for array index i .

keywords.name: fluid-density
keywords.description: Fluid mass density in zone (only available if model configure fluid has been specified)

keywords.name: state
keywords.description: The plasticity indicators for tetrahedrons are set to i . Normally the value of i used is 0 in order to reset the failure indicators.

keywords.name: stress-zz
keywords.description: Full stress state, specified by component. A component that is not specified is assumed to be zero. This command should be used after assigning a constitutive model. xx -component of stress. xy -component of stress. xz -component of stress. yy -component of stress. yz -component of stress. zz -component of stress.

keywords.name: stress-principal-dip-direction-minimum
keywords.description: Principal stress states, specified by value and by direction using the available keywords. Note that at least two directions must be specified, and magnitudes not specified are assumed to be zero. Note that the [zoneinitializeblock] is available after each of the following keywords, allowing them to be varied in space independently. This command should be used after assigning a constitutive model. Direction of intermediate principal stress. Direction of maximum principal stress. Direction of minimum principal stress. Intermediate principal stress magnitude. Maximum (most postitive) principal stress. Minimum (most negative) principal stress. Set dip of maximum principal stress. Dip and dip-direction can be given instead of the vector orientation. Orientations only need to be specified for two principal components. Set dip direction of maximum principal stress. Dip and dip-direction can be given instead of the vector orientation. Orientations only need to be specified for two principal components. Set dip of intermediate principal stress. Dip and dip-direction can be given instead of the vector orientation. Orientations only need to be specified for two principal components. Set dip direction of intermediate principal stress. Dip and dip-direction can be given instead of the vector orientation. Orientations only need to be specified for two principal components. Set dip of minimum principal stress. Dip and dip-direction can be given instead of the vector orientation. Orientations only need to be specified for two principal components. Set dip direction of minimum principal stress. Dip and dip-direction can be given instead of the vector orientation. Orientations only need to be specified for two principal directions.

keywords.name: stress-xx
keywords.description: xx -component of stress (See the description above for the difference between zone initialize stress-xx and zone initialize stress xx and for the next five keywords).

keywords.name: add
keywords.description: Add the specified value to the existing value.

keywords.name: gradient
keywords.description: Apply a gradient to the scalar-value provided. Note the origin keyword is optional, however, the default value (0,0,0) is used if the origin keyword is not included in the command — which may not produce the intended result.

keywords.name: multiply
keywords.description: Multiply the existing value by the specified value.

keywords.name: vary
keywords.description: Apply a linear variation to the scalar-value provided.
